top.guard: Mode guard: reverting to {{to}} in {{sec}}s — confirm you still have access.
top.tunNeedsRoot: TUN needs elevated privileges (start with sudo)
top.routingGlobalTip: Global: default-deny OFF — unlisted traffic egresses via proxy
top.globalBanner: Global routing: whitelist default-deny is OFF — unlisted traffic egresses via proxy (blacklist / threat-intel / process·device gates still apply). Switch back to Rule to restore default-deny.

settings.languageDesc: Console display language (saved in this browser).

acls.desc: Access control: what egress is allowed (whitelist) and what is explicitly denied (blacklist). Deny always wins over allow. No-Proxy picks the egress (direct vs proxy) — it never grants access.

whitelist.desc: Default-deny egress allow-list. Domains & IPs are destinations; Processes & Devices are opt-in source gates (empty = off).
whitelist.domainsHint: Suffix by default (example.com covers its subdomains). Wildcards: *.example.com (subdomains), foo* (prefix).
whitelist.processesHint: When non-empty, unknown binaries can't egress at all — even to a whitelisted destination.
whitelist.devicesHint: When non-empty, only these source IPs/CIDRs may egress. For gateway/router deployments.

blacklist.desc: Explicit egress deny-list. These destinations are REJECTED even if a whitelist or allow rule-set would otherwise permit them — the reject rules sit above every allow.
blacklist.domainsHint: Suffix by default (evil.com blocks its subdomains). Wildcards: *.evil.com, ads-* (prefix).
blacklist.keywordsHint: Matched as a substring anywhere in the domain.
blacklist.regexesHint: RE2 pattern matched against the domain (must compile).
blacklist.ipHint: Reject connections to these destination IPs/CIDRs.

directlist.desc: No-proxy (bypass) list: allowed traffic to these destinations egresses DIRECT instead of through the proxy. This is routing, not access — an entry must also be allowed (whitelisted or matched by an allow rule-set). Private/LAN ranges are always bypassed automatically.
directlist.domainsHint: Suffix by default (corp.local covers its subdomains). Wildcards: *.corp.local, intra-* (prefix).
directlist.ipHint: These destination IPs/CIDRs egress direct (never through the proxy).
